name: Armenian Lavash with Cheese and Herbs
steps: Warm the bread.
Fill with the cheese and herbs, and roll up.
steps_detailed: Warm the bread (standing in for lavash, Armenia's UNESCO-recognized thin bread) until soft and pliable.
Fill generously with the cheese, herbs, and cucumber, and roll up tightly - a simple, beloved Armenian breakfast.

name: Armenian Khachapuri-Style Egg Bread
steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water, and roll into a round.
Fill with the cheese, fold the edges up, and crack the egg into the center.
Bake at 425F for 15-18 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and water. Rest 20 minutes.
Roll into an oval, fill with the cheese, and fold the edges up into a boat shape, cracking the egg into the center.
Bake at 425F (220C) for 15-18 minutes until golden and the egg is just set - this cheese-and-egg bread reflects the shared culinary ground between Armenia and its Caucasus neighbors.

name: Armenian Spas

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Simmer the bulgur in 1 cup water until tender, about 15 minutes.
Whisk in the yogurt gently over low heat, without boiling.
Top with cilantro and serve warm.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Simmer the bulgur with the garlic in 1.25 cups water until tender, about 15-18 minutes.
Whisk in the yogurt gently over low heat, stirring constantly and never letting it boil, to prevent curdling.
Top with cilantro and the butter, and serve warm - spas, a soothing yogurt and wheat soup, is a traditional Armenian breakfast.

name: Armenian Itch (Eetch)
steps: Soak the bulgur in hot water 15-20 minutes until softened, then drain.
Toss with the tomatoes, onion, and lime juice.
Chill before serving.
steps_detailed: Soak the bulgur in hot water for 15-20 minutes until softened, then drain thoroughly.
Toss with the tomatoes, onion, cilantro, lime juice, and oil.
Chill at least 20 minutes before serving - itch, a bulgur salad similar to tabbouleh, is a beloved Armenian side dish.

name: Armenian Manti

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Make a simple dough from the flour and water, roll thin, and cut into small squares.
Fill with the beef and pinch into small boat shapes.
Bake at 400F for 20-25 minutes until golden, then simmer in broth or top with the garlic yogurt.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Make a firm dough from the flour, a pinch of salt, and water, and roll out thin, cutting into small squares.
Mix the beef with the onion and paprika, and place a small amount in the center of each square, pinching the ends up into small open boat shapes.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 22-28 minutes until golden, then top with the garlic yogurt - Armenian manti are traditionally baked rather than boiled, distinguishing them from many neighboring versions.

name: Armenian Topik

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Blend the chickpeas with the mashed potato until smooth.
Saute the onion until deeply golden, then fold in with the raisins.
Shape into small patties and chill before serving.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas with the mashed potato until smooth and cohesive.
Saute the onion in the oil until deeply golden and caramelized, 12-15 minutes, then fold into the chickpea mixture with the raisins and pistachios.

Shape into small patties or a log, and chill at least 30 minutes before slicing and serving - topik, a chickpea and potato dish, is an Armenian diaspora specialty, especially from Istanbul's Armenian community.

name: Armenian Harissa
steps: Simmer the chicken with the onion until very tender, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Add the bulgur to the broth and simmer 20-25 minutes, mashing frequently, until thick and porridge-like.
Stir the chicken back in, top with the butter, and serve.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion in 2.5 cups water for 25-30 minutes until very tender, then shred it finely, reserving the broth.
Add the bulgur (standing in for the hulled wheat traditionally used) to the broth and simmer 25-32 minutes, mashing frequently, until it forms a thick, smooth porridge.

Stir the shredded chicken back in, top with the butter and a dusting of paprika, and serve - harissa, one of Armenia's most ancient dishes, is traditionally cooked for hours in a communal pot during village festivals.

name: Armenian Khorovats
steps: Mix the lamb with the grated onion, paprika, and lime juice.
Shape into patties and grill until well charred and cooked through.
Serve with flatbread.
steps_detailed: Mix the lamb thoroughly with the grated onion, paprika, lime juice, garlic, and cilantro, and let marinate at least 20 minutes.
Shape into patties or thread onto skewers, and grill over medium-high heat, 4-6 minutes per side, until well charred and cooked through.
Serve with flatbread - khorovats, Armenian barbecue, is a cornerstone of Armenian gatherings and celebrations.

name: Armenian Ghapama

ingredients.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Toss the pumpkin chunks with the rice, raisins, and honey.
Bake at 375F for 35-40 minutes until tender.

ingredients_full.note: small pumpkin, top cut off and seeds scooped out, or chunked if a whole small pumpkin isn't practical

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Parboil the basmati 8-9 minutes until still firm, then mix with the raisins, walnuts, apricots, honey, and cinnamon.
Pack the filling into the hollowed pumpkin, replace the lid, and bake at 375F (190C) for 50-60 minutes, until the pumpkin flesh is fully tender and the filling is cooked through.
Slice into wedges to serve, with pumpkin and filling together in each piece.

Ghapama, a whole pumpkin stuffed with sweet spiced rice, is traditionally brought to the table intact and cut open there - real pumpkin is what makes this recognizably ghapama, since the dish's whole presentation depends on using the vegetable as its own serving vessel.

name: Armenian Dolma
steps: Mix the raw rice with the beef and tomatoes for the filling.
Wrap small portions in the grape leaves, rolling tightly.
Simmer in water to cover for 40-45 minutes until the rice is tender.
steps_detailed: Mix the raw rice with the beef, tomatoes, cilantro, and cinnamon for the filling.
Lay each grape leaf flat, place a spoonful of filling near the stem end, fold in the sides, and roll tightly.
Arrange the rolls snugly in a pot, cover with water, and simmer 42-50 minutes until the rice and beef are fully cooked.
Armenian dolma, often flavored with warm spices like cinnamon, is a beloved dish for family gatherings.

name: Armenian Beef and Apricot Stew

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef with the onion.
Add the apricots and tomatoes with a splash of water, and simmer, covered, 1.25-1.5 hours until tender.
Serve with rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, and cinnamon over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the dried apricots, diced tomatoes, and 3/4 cup water.
Cover and simmer over low heat 1.25-1.75 hours until the beef is completely tender and the apricots have softened into the sauce.
Serve with rice - apricots are so closely tied to Armenia that the fruit's Latin name, Prunus armeniaca, references the country; this sweet-savory stew celebrates that heritage.

name: Armenian Hummus with Pomegranate

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, and lime juice with a splash of water until smooth.
Top with the pomegranate seeds and serve with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until very smooth.
Top generously with the pomegranate seeds - the jeweled garnish is a distinctly Armenian touch, pomegranate being deeply symbolic in Armenian culture.

name: Armenian Pomegranate and Walnut Salad
steps: Toss the pomegranate seeds with the walnuts and cilantro.
Serve fresh as a simple side.
steps_detailed: Toss the pomegranate seeds with the walnuts, cilantro, lime juice, and oil.
A simple, jewel-bright salad celebrating two of Armenia's most treasured ingredients.

name: Armenian Gata Savory Twist
steps: Make a rich, buttery dough from the flour and butter.
Roll thin, sprinkle with crumbled cheese, and roll up into a log.
Slice and bake at 375F for 18-22 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Work the butter into the flour with a pinch of salt and a little cold water to form a rich, short dough.
Roll out thin, sprinkle generously with the crumbled cheese, and roll up tightly into a log.
Slice into rounds, brush with the egg wash, and bake at 375F (190C) for 20-25 minutes until golden.
This nods to gata, Armenia's beloved layered pastry, in a savory cheese-filled variation.

name: Armenian Su Boregi
steps: Boil sheets of phyllo briefly, then layer with the melted butter and cheese mixed with egg.
Bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes until golden.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Briefly boil sheets of phyllo (an unusual step that gives su boregi, meaning 'water borek,' its distinctive soft, layered texture) for 30-45 seconds each, then drain.
Layer the boiled sheets with the melted butter, and the cheese mixed with the egg and yogurt.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 25-32 minutes until golden - su boregi is a beloved Armenian savory pastry, especially popular at gatherings.

name: Armenian Gata
steps: Work the butter and half the sugar into the flour to form a rich dough.
Roll thin, sprinkle with the remaining sugar, and roll up into a log.
Slice, brush with egg wash, and bake at 375F for 18-22 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Work the butter, half the sugar, and cinnamon into the flour with a little water to form a rich, short dough.
Roll out thin, sprinkle generously with the remaining sugar, and roll up tightly into a log.
Slice into rounds, brush with the egg wash, and bake at 375F (190C) for 20-25 minutes until deeply golden.
Gata, a sweet layered pastry bread, is one of Armenia's most iconic desserts.

name: Armenian Pakhlava
steps: Layer the phyllo with the melted butter, scattering the chopped walnuts between layers.
Bake at 350F for 25-30 minutes until deeply golden.
Pour the honey over while still warm.
steps_detailed: Layer sheets of phyllo in a small buttered dish, brushing each with melted butter, scattering the walnuts mixed with cinnamon between layers.
Cut into diamond shapes before baking.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 28-35 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
Pour the honey evenly over the hot pakhlava as soon as it comes out of the oven - Armenian pakhlava, distinguished by its all-walnut filling, is a treasured holiday dessert.

name: Armenian Dried Apricot and Walnut Sweets
steps: Split each apricot and press a walnut half into the center.
Drizzle with honey and serve.
steps_detailed: Split each dried apricot and press a walnut half into the center.
Drizzle with the honey and a light dusting of cinnamon - a simple, elegant Armenian sweet built on the country's most celebrated fruit.

name: Armenian Garlic Yogurt Sauce

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Whisk the yogurt with the garlic and cilantro.
Season with salt and serve alongside khorovats or manti.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Whisk the yogurt with the garlic, cilantro, and lime juice until smooth.
Season with salt and chill briefly - this garlic yogurt sauce is essential alongside Armenian manti and grilled meats.

name: Armenian Pomegranate Sauce
steps: Simmer the pomegranate juice with the sugar for 8-10 minutes until reduced and syrupy.
Drizzle over grilled meats or salads.
steps_detailed: Simmer the pomegranate juice with the sugar over medium-low heat for 10-12 minutes until reduced by about half and syrupy, finishing with the lime juice.
Drizzle over grilled meats or salads - pomegranate's tangy sweetness is central to Armenian cooking.
